content.full_text:
CITY OF ROHNERT PARK
6750 Commerce Boulevard
Rohnert Park, Sonoma County, California 94927
Telephone 795-2411

January 23, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Eighth Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for opinion

Dear Sir or Madam:

This will constitute a request for an opinion on the question of
whether a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board is in a
conflict of interest for reviewing the decision of a hearing
examiner who decided an application for increase of rent in
Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park where the member is a tenant.

I

The Facts

Rancho Verde is one of five (5) mobilehome parks within the City
of Rohnert Park. It has approximately 300 spaces. The other
mobilehome parks and the number of spaces are:

    Las Casitas                126 Spaces
    Rancho Feliz              294 Spaces
    Rancho Grande             314 Spaces
    Valley Village            286 Spaces

1. On or about January 31, 1991, the owner of Rancho Verde
Mobilehome Park applied for permission to increase individual
rentals at Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park by an additional $220
more or less. The increase was to be effective as of April 1,
1991.

2. On or about February 7, 1991, the Rohnert Park Rent
Review Board, in accordance with Section 3. of the Rohnert Park
Rent Review Ordinance, (photocopy enclosed) which had been
adopted through the initiative process, appointed Mark Dennis,
Esq., to hear the rent increase application. Authority for such
action is found in Section 7.02 C. of the Rules and Regulations
which were adopted pursuant to Section 3. G. of the ordinance and
which accompany the Rent Review Ordinance. A copy of Chapter 7
of the Rules and Regulations is attached hereto. Reference to
specific sections of the Rules and Regulations is made when
applicable.

January 23, 1992
Page 2

Mr. Dennis held the hearings and the Rent Review Board, as
provided by the Rent Review Ordinance and the Rules and
Regulations, directed that the hearing be reopened for
resolution of issues or questions submitted by the Board. Rules
and Regulations §7.15 D. 1. Ms. Coe was not a member of the
Rohnert Park Rent Review Board at this time.

Mr. Dennis conducted a limited rehearing and submitted his
decision. The decision was reviewed by the Board in accordance
with Rules and Regulations §7.14 D. Mr. Dennis's decision
reduced the request from $220 to approximately $80 per month.
Ms. Coe was a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board at the
time of the second review.

When the item was presented to the Board, the attorney for Rancho
Verde challenged Ms. Coe's ability to participate in the review
because Ms. Coe was a tenant in Rancho Verde.

I issued the attached opinion to the Board which finds that Ms.
Coe does not have a conflict.

The Rohnert Park Rent Review Board has directed me to request an
opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission as to
whether Ms. Coe has a conflict. Rules & Regulation §7.02 D.
addresses conflict of interest matters.

If the opinion is that Ms. Coe does have a conflict, I should
like to know the extent of the conflict and to what situations like it
may apply, as well and as far as the issuer of the FPPC opinion
can opine. For example:

1. If Ms. Coe considers an issue that concerns all of the
five mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park is Ms. Coe precluded from
participating in the decision?

2. If Ms. Coe considers an issue that affects some, but not
all of the mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park but Rancho Verde is
not involved in the decision, does such participation and
decision making constitute a conflict?

3. Same question, only assume Rancho Verde is one of the
affected parks.

4. Is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in any decision
that affects Rancho Verde only?

5. If the answer to question number four is no, under what
circumstances is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in such
discussions and any decision that might issue therefrom?

January 23, 1992
Page 3

It is possible that the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board could
designate itself to hear a mobilehome park owner's application
for rental increases. Rules and Regulations § 7.02 C, 7.15 D. 4.
I believe that issue should be addressed as well. As a tenant in
Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park and if the Rohnert Park Rent Review
Board elects to hear a rent increase application, is Ms. Coe
precluded from hearing such an application regardless of the park
from which it originates?  Again, if the answer is no, under
what circumstances or what guidelines are available to determine
which applications are proper for Ms. Coe to hear and which are
not?

Finally, and although it did not arise in the situation outlined,
a mobilehome park owner has asked the hypothetical question of
what conflict of interest constraints would be placed upon a
mobilehome park owner if he or she was appointed to the Rent
Review Board?  Would the same general prohibitions apply to a
mobilehome park owner relative to his or her park as apply to a
tenant regarding his or her park or do different standards or
guidelines apply for mobilehome park owners who sit on mobile
home rent review boards?

Thank you for your considered opinion in this matter.

Very truly yours,,

JOHN D. FLITNER
City Attorney

JDF/cc

Enclosures

sections.question: Whether a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board is in conflict of interest for reviewing the decision of a hearing examiner who decided an application for increase of rent in Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park where the member is a tenant. Specifically: (1) If Ms. Coe considers an issue that concerns all five mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park, is she precluded from participating? (2) If Ms. Coe considers an issue affecting some, but not all mobilehome parks where Rancho Verde is not involved, does such participation constitute a conflict? (3) Same as (2) but where Rancho Verde is one of the affected parks. (4) Is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in any decision that affects Rancho Verde only? (5) If not, under what circumstances is she precluded? (6) If the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board elects to hear a rent increase application, is Ms. Coe precluded from hearing such an application regardless of which park it originates from? (7) What conflict of interest constraints would apply to a mobilehome park owner appointed to the Rent Review Board relative to his or her park, and do different standards apply compared to tenants?

Thank you for your considered opinion in this natter.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Rent Review Board member who is a tenant in a specific mobilehome park (Rancho Verde) has a conflict of interest when reviewing rent decisions involving that park, and under what circumstances such conflicts arise for similar situations and for mobilehome park owners on the Board.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed factual background about a rent review proceeding and multiple specific questions regarding conflict of interest applicability. The letter references a prior opinion issued by the city attorney but requests FPPC guidance. Document is from 1992 and uses older formatting conventions.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board is in conflict of interest for reviewing the decision of a hearing examiner who decided an application for increase of rent in Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park where the member is a tenant. Specifically: (1) If Ms. Coe considers an issue that concerns all five mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park, is she precluded from participating? (2) If Ms. Coe considers an issue affecting some, but not all mobilehome parks where Rancho Verde is not involved, does such participation constitute a conflict? (3) Same as (2) but where Rancho Verde is one of the affected parks. (4) Is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in any decision that affects Rancho Verde only? (5) If not, under what circumstances is she precluded? (6) If the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board elects to hear a rent increase application, is Ms. Coe precluded from hearing such an application regardless of which park it originates from? (7) What conflict of interest constraints would apply to a mobilehome park owner appointed to the Rent Review Board relative to his or her park, and do different standards apply compared to tenants?

embedding.summary: This is a request from the Rohnert Park City Attorney to the FPPC for an opinion on whether a Rent Review Board member (Ms. Coe) who is a tenant at Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park has a conflict of interest in reviewing a rent increase decision for that park, with detailed questions about the scope and applicability of conflict restrictions in various scenarios.
